slides.title: Who Should Use It, When, and for What Decisions
slides.subtitle: Western Digital Human-Machine Collaboration Playbook, Section 2

slides.title: Who Should Use It, When, and for What Decisions

slides.section_title: Who Should Use It, When, and for What Decisions
slides.title: This playbook is designed for managers who make or shape process-evolution decisions
left_block_body: Directors and senior directors who own process performance, productivity, quality, or transformation outcomes
Vice presidents and functional leaders shaping automation, digital, or operating-model priorities
Cross-functional managers coordinating decisions across operations, engineering, IT, analytics, HR, or finance
Plant or site leaders balancing performance, workforce, and implementation tradeoffs
right_block_body: Own or influence process redesign decisions
Decide where automation or AI-enabled decision support is worth pursuing
Align stakeholders across functions, systems, and budgets
Translate transformation ambition into scalable operating choices

slides.section_title: Who Should Use It, When, and for What Decisions
slides.title: Use the playbook when a team is deciding whether, where, and how a process should evolve
block_body: Evaluating a process redesign opportunity because of bottlenecks, handoff issues, quality losses, or management pain points
Considering automation or AI-enabled decision support for part of a workflow
Assessing readiness for a new digital initiative where data quality, systems integration, governance, or ownership remain unclear
Prioritizing whether to pilot, scale, redesign first, or defer an opportunity
Planning workforce implications, including role redesign, skill needs, escalation logic, and accountability

slides.section_title: Who Should Use It, When, and for What Decisions
slides.title: The playbook helps leaders make structured choices about target state, readiness, prioritization, and transition path
dominant_block_body: Target-state decisions: what human-machine collaboration model best fits the process
Readiness decisions: whether the process is ready to move, and what gaps exist across data, systems, governance, capability, and ownership
Prioritization decisions: whether to pilot, scale, redesign first, or pause
Transition decisions: what role redesign, capability building, governance, funding, and cross-functional coordination would be required
secondary_block_body: A set of feasible future-state options
A structured view of tradeoffs, prerequisites, and risks
A basis for management to choose a target state and define the transition path
